title: itertools: concurrent repr() of a shared count() races count_repr's plain read of lz->cnt against count_next's atomic advance
description: In the free-threaded build count_next advances a fast-mode count()'s counter lz->cnt with an atomic compare-exchange (itertoolsmodule.c:3599), but count_repr reads the same field with a plain non-atomic load (itertoolsmodule.c:3612). Sharing a count() across threads while some repr() it and others next() it is a data race (atomic write vs plain read) on an operation callers treat as read-only.
sites: Modules/itertoolsmodule.c count_repr:3612 (plain read of lz->cnt in fast-mode repr branch)
race_kind: atomic/non-atomic mismatch (writer atomic, reader plain) on a shared counter field
shared_state: countobject.cnt (Py_ssize_t fast-mode counter; written atomically by count_next, read plainly by count_repr)
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; aligned Py_ssize_t, no torn read; worst case is a stale repr value; technically UB data race on read-only-looking repr())
suggested_fix: read lz->cnt in count_repr via _Py_atomic_load_ssize_relaxed(&lz->cnt) (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_SSIZE_RELAXED), matching the relaxed atomic load count_next already uses at itertoolsmodule.c:3591
notes: Real bug: incomplete free-threading atomic conversion of the count object. count_next was hardened (relaxed-atomic load + CAS on lz->cnt, plus a critical section for the slow-mode fast->slow transition), but count_repr's fast-mode read of lz->cnt was left as a plain load. TSan's SUMMARY top-frame alternates between count_repr:3612 and count_next:3599/_Py_atomic_compare_exchange_ssize depending on scheduling; same race. Fleet vehicle drove the write side via list(count()) (list_extend_iter -> count_next); the minimal repro uses next() instead to avoid the infinite list(). Audit other itertools objects for the same atomic-write/plain-read asymmetry. FILED from umbrella #153852 -> python/cpython#153908 (FIXED (merged PR python/cpython#153917; 3.14/3.15 backports #153952/#153951 open)) [2026-07-18]. FLEET-08 (2026-07-18): fusil's op-h shares a BIG-INT count(10**18, 2), which drives the SLOW mode (lz->long_cnt, a PyObject*, not the fast lz->cnt ssize). count_repr plain-BORROWS lz->long_cnt and reprs it (PyUnicode_FromFormat '%R') while count_nextlong writes lz->long_cnt = stepped_up and returns the OLD long_cnt (ref transferred to the next() caller, which then DECREFs it). So besides the plain pointer race `count_nextlong | count_repr` (142 veh), count_repr's PyObject_Repr on the borrowed old long_cnt USE-AFTER-FREES it when the caller frees it -> 3 UAF faces (`_Py_TYPE_impl | _PyMem_DebugRawAlloc` 7 veh, `_Py_TYPE_impl | _PyMem_DebugRawFree` 1, `long_to_decimal_string_internal | _PyMem_DebugRawFree` 1) -- all have count_repr on the stack (frame ~#6). CAVEAT: those 3 faces are GENERIC-shaped (Py_TYPE / long repr vs obmalloc DebugRaw); a future NON-count repr-UAF would mislabel as TSAN-0006, so verify count_repr is on the stack. The fleet-08 build predated #153917 (no _Py_atomic in count). **INCOMPLETE-FIX RESIDUAL CONFIRMED 2026-07-18 on a POST-#153917 build** (debug-ft-nojit-tsan rebuilt at main a1d580430c8, which contains the count fix commit 5200f119242 'gh-153908: Fix data race in itertools.count.__repr__ (GH-153917)'). #153917 made count_next fully safe (fast mode = _Py_atomic CAS on lz->cnt; slow mode = count_nextlong under Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(lz)) and count_repr FAST-mode safe (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_SSIZE_RELAXED(lz->cnt)) -- but count_repr's SLOW-mode path was left unguarded: its very first access `if (lz->long_cnt == NULL)` (itertoolsmodule.c:3678) plain-reads the lz->long_cnt POINTER with no critical section, racing count_nextlong's `lz->long_cnt = stepped_up` write (:3639, which runs under the CS). A critical section only serializes vs OTHER critical sections, so count_repr (which never takes it) still races. REPRODUCED deterministically: `count(10**18, 2)` (big-int -> slow mode) hammered by next()+repr() across 8 threads -> exit 66, `count_nextlong:3639 | count_repr:3678`; the fast-mode `count()` control is now CLEAN. (The UAF face -- count_repr borrows long_cnt and reprs it while count_nextlong replaces + the next() caller frees it -- is the downstream consequence of the same unguarded read; TSan halts on the plain data race first.) Fix: take Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(lz) around count_repr's long_cnt access (as count_next's slow path does), or read long_cnt via an atomic. repro_slowmode_residual.py + slowmode_residual_backtrace.txt saved. WORTH a follow-up note on #153908/#153917 (user's call on filing).

related_issues.note: Modules/itertoolsmodule.c is UNCHECKED on the gh-116738 audit list; count_repr reads lz->cnt plainly (count_next uses a CAS) on current main.

assessment: real, new — count_next CAS hardened but count_repr reader missed; count not covered by the above
fleet02_notes: fleet-02 variant: the count_next CAS write-side vs count_repr read (same race, parser picked the atomic frame)
fleet10_note: Added the generic-shaped UAF face `Include/internal/pycore_long.h:_PyLong_DigitCount | Objects/obmalloc.c:_PyMem_DebugRawAlloc` (fleet-10, 1 veh). Same count slow-mode use-after-free downstream of the #153917 residual: count_repr borrows lz->long_cnt and PyObject_Repr's it (-> _PyLong_DigitCount / long_to_decimal_string) while count_nextlong replaces long_cnt and the next() caller frees the old int, whose storage the allocator (_PyMem_DebugRawAlloc) then reuses. CAVEAT (as with the other generic faces): verify count_repr is on the stack before trusting this label -- confirmed present here (read side frame #8 count_repr:3696).
